diagrams.description: Developmental Psychology is the scientific study of how and why humans change over the course of their life. This flowchart shows its framework: the ultimate goal is to describe, explain, and optimize human growth across physical, cognitive, and socioemotional domains. Research builds on major theoretical perspectives—psychoanalytic (Freud, Erikson), learning (Skinner, Bandura), cognitive‑developmental (Piaget, Vygotsky), ethological (Bowlby, Ainsworth), and ecological (Bronfenbrenner). These guide diverse methods (longitudinal, cross‑sectional, sequential, observations) that uncover patterns of continuity and discontinuity, critical and sensitive periods, and the interplay of nature and nurture. The resulting knowledge informs applications in education, parenting, clinical intervention, and social policy.
